content.assessment: This proposal is the only one that directly confronts the core institutional finding that village governments hold no formal authority over closure (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01) by proposing statutory procedure, consultation rights, and mitigation — a genuine governance change rather than operation within existing discretion. It also implicitly acknowledges that current closure criteria (efficiency/enrollment ratios, F-live-hungarian_education_system-03) continue to be applied without procedural rigor, and that the 2026 reform (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07) will restructure but not abolish multi-tier centralized authority — meaning statutory mitigation would need to be designed to interlock with, not replace, that retained tier structure. The proposal does not address national funding-formula pressure (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04) or the pre-existing 38% coverage gap (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06), focusing instead on procedural fairness for cases still undergoing closure.
opportunities: Statutory reform could be timed to coincide with and reinforce the 2026 governance restructuring, potentially embedding new procedural safeguards into the revised tankerület framework (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07)
strengths: Directly targets the institutional gap in village-level authority by proposing statutory consultation rights, addressing a documented structural absence (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01)

Compatible in principle with the incoming 2026 reform's retained multi-tier centralized closure authority, since procedural guarantees can be layered onto an unchanged tier structure (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07)
threats: Formalizing closure procedure without changing underlying enrollment/efficiency criteria (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03) risks legitimizing continued closures under a veneer of process rather than altering substantive outcomes
Kindergarten-level enrollment thresholds operating on the same logic (F-live-hungarian_education_system-08) are outside this proposal's scope, leaving a parallel access problem unaddressed
weaknesses: Does not alter the underlying national funding formula that generates fiscal pressure independent of governance form (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04)

Does not address the pre-existing coverage gap of 38% of settlements already lacking institutions, since it is a procedure for future closures, not a restoration mechanism (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06)
